# deletion guards (task rm + note rm) refuse a referenced target; show refs/backlinks
mkdir proj
cd proj
exec mtt init
stdout 'initialized'
exec mtt add 'A'
stdout 'created t1'
exec mtt add 'B'
stdout 'created t2'
exec mtt note add spec
stdout 'created spec'

# t2 and note spec both reference t1
exec mtt ref add t2 task:t1
exec mtt note ref add spec task:t1

# show t2 renders a refs: section
exec mtt show t2
stdout '  refs:'
stdout 'task:t1  \[ok\]'

# show t1 renders a backlinks: section (from the note AND the task, carrier-sorted)
exec mtt show t1
stdout '  backlinks:'
stdout 'note:spec'
stdout 'task:t2'

# task rm t1 is refused (referenced by t2 and note spec)
! exec mtt rm t1
stderr 'referenced by'
exec mtt show t1
stdout 't1'

# --force without who/why -> exit 2
! exec mtt rm t1 --force
stderr 'missing required attribution'

# --force with who/why deletes (leaves the refs dangling)
exec mtt rm t1 --force --who me --why cleanup
stdout 'removed t1'

# note rm guard: make note spec referenced by t2, then refuse its delete
exec mtt ref add t2 note:spec
! exec mtt note rm spec
stderr 'referenced by'
! exec mtt note rm spec --force
stderr 'missing required attribution'
exec mtt note rm spec --force --who me --why cleanup
stdout 'removed spec'

# a self-referencing note is NOT blocked by its own ref
exec mtt note add solo
exec mtt note ref add solo note:solo
exec mtt note rm solo
stdout 'removed solo'
